title: Asset Decommission Verification
spl:
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:change_request" state="Closed" earliest=-30d
| where match(lower(short_description),"(?i)decom|retire") OR lower(category)="retire"
| rename cmdb_ci as ci_sys_id
| join type=left max=1 ci_sys_id [ search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:cmdb_ci" | rename sys_id as ci_sys_id | table ci_sys_id, install_status, operational_status ]
| where install_status!="retired" AND lower(operational_status)!="retired" AND lower(operational_status)!="non-operational"
| table number, short_description, ci_sys_id, install_status, operational_status
description: Confirms decommissioned servers no longer appear in monitoring, AD, or hypervisor inventory ‚Äî reducing zombie assets and license bleed.
value: Confirms decommissioned servers no longer appear in monitoring, AD, or hypervisor inventory ‚Äî reducing zombie assets and license bleed.
implementation: Map `cmdb_ci` (or task CI list) from the change; normalize `install_status`/`operational_status` values to your CMDB. Optionally join inventory (`vmware:inv:vm`) on hostname to catch VMs still present. Drive cleanup when decom CHG is closed but CI not retired.
visualization: Table (failed decom verification), Single value (open exceptions), Bar chart (by team).
knownFalsePositives: Shifts in ticket and workflow data during large incidents, process or field changes, holiday coverage, and after big rollouts; spot-check a sample in the source system before you treat the trend as a people problem.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Monitoring + CMDB.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: Decommission change tickets, `snow:cmdb_ci`, `vmware:inv:vm`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Map `cmdb_ci` (or task CI list) from the change; normalize `install_status`/`operational_status` values to your CMDB. Optionally join inventory (`vmware:inv:vm`) on hostname to catch VMs still present. Drive cleanup when decom CHG is closed but CI not retired.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:change_request" state="Closed" earliest=-30d
| where match(lower(short_description),"(?i)decom|retire") OR lower(category)="retire"
| rename cmdb_ci as ci_sys_id
| join type=left max=1 ci_sys_id [ search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:cmdb_ci" | rename sys_id as ci_sys_id | table ci_sys_id, install_status, operational_status ]
| where install_status!="retired" AND lower(operational_status)!="retired" AND lower(operational_status)!="non-operational"
| table number, short_description, ci_sys_id, install_status, operational_status
```

Understanding this SPL

**Asset Decommission Verification** ‚Äî Confirms decommissioned servers no longer appear in monitoring, AD, or hypervisor inventory ‚Äî reducing zombie assets and license bleed.

Documented **Data sources**: Decommission change tickets, `snow:cmdb_ci`, `vmware:inv:vm`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Monitoring + CMDB. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:change_request. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="snow:change_request", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where match(lower(short_description),"(?i)decom|retire") OR lower(category)="retire"` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Renames fields with `rename` for clarity or joins.
‚Ä¢ Joins to a subsearch with `join` ‚Äî set `max=` to match cardinality and avoid silent truncation.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where install_status!="retired" AND lower(operational_status)!="retired" AND lower(operational_status)!="non-operational"` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **Asset Decommission Verification**): table number, short_description, ci_sys_id, install_status, operational_status


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare with the ServiceNow change request list or form for the same records. Verify change numbers, states, and timing against our search. Use the same filters you expect operations to use in the list view.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (failed decom verification), Single value (open exceptions), Bar chart (by team).
grandmaExplanation: We help prove a thing is really out of use and not still billing or still reachable, so retire and risk stay aligned.
